reasoning: The Lean statement sets O to the set of open subsets and C to the set of closed subsets of the metric space M, then asserts the existence of a bijective function f : O → C. Using Set-as-subtype coercion, O → C is a function from the subtype of open sets to the subtype of closed sets, and Bijective f asserts injectivity and surjectivity. This matches the informal claim of a bijection between the collection of open subsets and the collection of closed subsets. No extraneous hypotheses are added and no hypotheses of the informal statement are dropped.
